name: Hepatectomy / liver resection anatomy
review_note: Standard anatomy; not yet clinician-verified. Confirm the transection plane matches the requested resection.

figure_scopes.overview: Liver orientation with a segment/sector map and the transection plane
figure_scopes.storyboard: Segment map -> inflow/outflow landmarks -> transection plane -> resection territory/remnant
figure_scopes.closeup: Transection plane tied to hepatic vein/portal anatomy

laterality: Hepatic vein planes define boundaries; IVC remains posterior; caudate (Segment I) sits posterior near the IVC
must_have: Liver sectors/segments relevant to the requested resection, hepatic veins, portal pedicles, IVC relationship, and transection plane.
Gallbladder fossa and ligamentum teres landmarks when an anterior orientation is used.
spatial_rules: Hepatic vein planes define intersegmental/intersectional boundaries.
Portal pedicles supply segments and should not be drawn as hepatic vein branches.
IVC remains posterior; Segment I/caudate sits posterior near the IVC.

forbidden_errors.error: Portal vein and hepatic vein systems swapped.
Caudate lobe placed on the anterior free surface.
A random transection plane unrelated to segment/section anatomy.

safety_concept: Tie the transection plane to hepatic vein/portal anatomy; do not confuse portal inflow with hepatic venous outflow.
phases: Transection plane tied to hepatic vein/portal anatomy.
Resection territory or remnant anatomy, if requested.
verify: Portal inflow and hepatic venous outflow are not confused.
IVC and caudate relationship is posterior and coherent.
